author: Selections from the Hymns and Homilies of Ephraim the Syrian and from the Demonstrations of Aphrahat the Persian Sage
book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Selections from the Hymns and Homilies of Ephraim the Syrian and from the Demonstrations of Aphrahat the Persian Sage.
Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.

metadata.author: Selections from the Hymns and Homilies of Ephraim the Syrian and from the Demonstrations of Aphrahat the Persian Sage

ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: Hymn III.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Blessed be that first day of thine, Lord, wherewith this day of Thy Feast is stamped!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day is like Thee, in that it shows mercy unto men, in that it is handed down and comes with all generations.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: This is the day that ends with the aged, and returns that it may begin with the young! a day that by its love refreshes itself, that it may refresh by its might us decayed creatures.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day when it had visited us and passed, and gone away, in its mercy returned and visited us again: for it knows that human nature needs it; in all things like unto Thee as seeking us.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The world is in want of its fountain; and for it, Lord, as for Thee, all therein are athirst.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: This is the day that rules over the seasons! the dominion of Thy day is like Thine, which stretches over generations that have come, and are to come!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day is like unto Thee, because when it is one, it buds and multiplies itself, that it may be like Thee!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In this Thy day, Lord, which is near unto us, we see Thy Birth that is far off!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Like to Thee be Thy day to us, Lord; let it be a mediator and a warranter of peace.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day reconciled Heaven and earth, because therein the Highest came down to the lowest.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day was able to reconcile the Just One, who was wroth at our sins; Thy day forgave thousands of sins, for in it bowels of mercy shone forth upon the guilty!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Great, Lord, is Thy day; let it not be small upon us, let it show mercy according as it used to do, upon us transgressors!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: And if every day, Lord, Thy forgiveness wells forth, how exceeding great should it be upon this day!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: All the days from the Treasure of Thy bright day gain blessings.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: All the feasts from the stores of this feast have their fairness and their ornaments.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy bowels of mercy upon Thy day make Thou to abound unto us, O Lord!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Make us to distinguish Thy day from all days! for great is the treasure-house of the day of Thy Birth; let it be the ransomer of debtors!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Great is this day above all days, for in it came forth mercy to sinners.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: A store of medicines is this Thy great day, because on it shone forth the Medicine of Life to the wounded!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: A treasure of helpful graces is this day, for that on it Light gleamed forth upon our blindness!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Yea, it also brought a sheaf unto us; and it came, that from it might flow plenty upon our hunger.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: This day is that forerunning Cluster, in which the cup of salvation was concealed!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: This day is the first-born feast, which, being born the first, overcomes all feasts.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In the winter which strips the fruit of the branches off from the barren vine, Fruit sprang up unto us; in the cold that bares all the trees, a shoot was green for us of the house of Jesse.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In December when the seed is hidden in the earth, there sprouted forth from the Womb the Ear of Life.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In March when the seed was sprouting in the air, a Sheaf sowed itself in the earth.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The harvest thereof, Death devoured it in Hell; which the Medicine of life that is hidden therein did yet burst open!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In March when the lambs bleat in the wilderness, into the Womb the Paschal Lamb entered!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Out of the stream whence the fishers came up, He was baptized and came up Who incloses all things in his net; out of the stream the fish whereof Simon took, out of it the Fisher of men came up, and took him.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With the Cross which catches all robbers, He caught up unto life that robber!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Living by His death emptied Hell, He unloosed it and let fly away from it entire multitudes!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The publicans and harlots, the impure snares, the snares of the deceitful fowler the Holy One seized!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The sinful woman, who was a snare for men, He made a mirror for penitent women!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The fig that cast its fruit, that refused fruit, offered Zacchæus as fruit; the fruit of its own nature it gave not, but it yielded one reasonable fruit!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Lord spread His thirst over the well, and caught her that was thirsty with the water that He asked of her.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He caught one soul at the well, and again caught with her the whole city: twelve fishers the Holy One caught, and again caught with them the whole world.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: As for Iscariot, that escaped from His nets, the strangling halter fell upon his neck!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: His all-quickening net catches the living, and he that escapes from it escapes from the living.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: And who is able, Lord, to tell me up the several succours that are hid in Thee?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: How shall the parched mouth be able to drink from the Fountain of the Godhead!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Answer today the voice of our petition; let our prayer which is in words take effect in deeds.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Heal us, O my Master; every time that we see Thy Feast, may it cause rumours that we have heard to pass away.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Our mind wanders amid these voices.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: O Voice of the Father, still [other] voices; the world is noisy, in Thee let it gain itself quiet; for by Thee the sea was stilled from its storms.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The devils rejoiced when they heard the voice of blasphemy: let the Watchers rejoice in us as they are wont.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: From amongst Thy fold there is the voice of sorrowfulness; O Thou that makest all rejoice, let Thy flock rejoice! as for our murmur, O my Master, in it reject us not: our mouth murmurs since it is sinful.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let Thy day, O Lord, give us all manner of joy, with the flowers of peace, let us keep Thy passover.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In the day of Thy Ascension we are lifted up: with the new Bread shall be the memorial thereof.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: O Lord, increase our peace, that we may keep three feasts of the Godhead.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Great is Thy day, Lord, let us not be despised.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: All men honour the day of Thy birth.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thou righteous One, keep Thou the glory of Thy birth; for even Herod honoured the day of His birth!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The dances of the impure one pleased the tyrant; to Thee, Lord, let the voice of chaste women be sweet!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thee, Lord, let the voice of chaste women please, whose bodies Thou guardest holily.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of Herod was like him: Thy day too is like Thee!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of the troubled one was troubled with sin; and fair as Thou art is Thy fair day!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The feast of the tyrant killed the preacher; in Thy feast every man preaches glory.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: On the day of the murderer, the Voice was put to silence; but on Thy day are the voices of the feast.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The foul one in his feast put out the Light, that darkness might cover the adulterers.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The season of the Holy One trims lamps, that darkness may flee with the hidden things thereof.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of that fox stank like himself; but holy is the feast of the True Lamb.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of the transgressor passed away like himself; Thy day like Thyself abideth for ever.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of the tyrant raged like himself, because with his chain it put to silence the righteous Voice.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The feast of the Meek One is tranquil like Himself, because His sun shines upon His persecutors.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The tyrant was conscious that He was not a king, therefore to the King of kings he gave place.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The whole day, Lord, suffices me not to balance Thy praise with his blame.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: May Thy Gracious day cause my sin to pass away, seeing that it is with the day of the impure one, that I have weighed Thy day!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For great is Thy day beyond comparison! nor can it be compared with our days.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The day of man is as of the earthy: the day of God is as of God!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thy day, Lord, is greater than those of the prophets, and I have taken and set it beside that of the murderer!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thou knowest, O Lord, as knowing all things, how to hear the comparison that my tongue hath made.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let Thy day grant our requests for life, since his day granted the request for death.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The needy king swore on his feast that half his kingdom should be the reward of the dance!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let Thy feast then, O Thou that enrichest all, shed down in mercy a crumb of fine wheat flour!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: From the dry land gushed the Fountain, which sufficed to satisfy the thirst of the Gentiles!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: From the Virgin’s womb as from a strong rock sprouted up the seed, whence was much fruit!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Barns without number did Joseph fill; and they were emptied and failed in the years of the famine.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: One true Sheaf gave bread; the bread of Heaven, whereof there is no stint.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The bread which the First-born brake in the wilderness, failed and passed away though very good.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He returned again and broke the New Bread which ages and generations shall not waste away!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The seven loaves also that He brake failed, and the five loaves too that He multiplied were consumed; the Bread that He brake exceeded the world’s needs, for the more it was divided, the more it multiplied exceedingly.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With much wine also He filled the waterpots; they drew it out, yet it failed though it was abundant: of the Cup that He gave though the draught was small, very great was its strength, so that there is no stint thereto.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: A Cup is He that contains all strong wines, and also a Mystery in the midst of which He Himself is!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The one Bread that He brake has no bound, and the one Cup that He mingled has no stint!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Wheat that was sown, on the third day came up and filled the Garner of Life.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The spiritual Bread, as the Giver of it, quickens the spiritual spiritually, and he that receives it carnally, receives it rashly to no profit.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: This Bread of grace let the spirit receive discerningly, as the medicine of Life.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: If the dead sacrifices in the name of devils were offered, yea eaten, not without a mystery; at the holy thing of the offering, how much more does it behove us that this mystery be circumspectly administered by us.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He that eateth of the sacrifice in the name of devils, becomes devilish without all contradiction.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He that eateth the Heavenly Bread, becomes Heavenly without doubt!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Wine teaches us, in that it makes him that is familiar therewith like itself: for it hates much him that is fond of it, and is intoxicating and maddening, and a mocker to him!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Light teaches us, in that it makes like unto itself the eye the daughter of the sun: the eye by the light saw the nakedness, and ran and chastely hid the chaste man.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: As for that nakedness it was wine that made it, which even to the chaste skills not to show mercy!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With the weapon of the deceiver the First-born clad Himself, that with the weapon that killed, He might restore to life again!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With the tree wherewith he slew us, He delivered us.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With the wine which maddened us, with it we were made chaste!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: With the rib that was drawn out of Adam, the wicked one drew out the heart of Adam.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: There rose from the Rib a hidden power, which cut off Satan as Dagon: for in that Ark a book was hidden that cried and proclaimed concerning the Conqueror!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: There was then a mystery revealed, in that Dagon was brought low in his own place of refuge!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The accomplishment came after the type, in that the wicked one was brought low in the place in which he trusted!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Blessed be He Who came and in Him were accomplished the mysteries of the left hand, and the right hand.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Fulfilled was the mystery that was in the Lamb, and fulfilled was the type that was in Dagon.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Blessed is He Who by the True Lamb redeemed us, and destroyed our destroyer as He did Dagon!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In December when the nights are long, rose unto us the Day, of Whom there is no bound!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In winter when all the world is gloomy, forth came the Fair One Who cheered all in the world!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In winter that makes the earth barren, virginity learned to bring forth.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In December, that causes the travails of the earth to cease, in it were the travails of virginity.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The early lamb no one ever used to see before the shepherds: and as for the true Lamb, in the season of His birth, the tidings of Him too hasted unto the shepherds.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: That old wolf saw the sucking Lamb, and he trembled before Him, though He had concealed himself; for because the wolf had put on sheep’s clothing, the Shepherd of all became a Lamb in the flocks, in order that when the greedy one had been bold against the Meek, the Mighty One might rend that Eater.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Holy One dwelt bodily in the womb; and He dwelt spiritually in the mind.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Mary that conceived Him abhorred the marriage bed; let not that soul commit whoredom in the which He dwelleth.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Because Mary perceived Him, she left her betrothed: He dwelleth in chaste virgins, if they perceive Him.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The deaf perceive not the mighty thunder, neither does the heady man the sound of the commandment.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For the deaf is bewildered in the time of the thunderclap, the heady man is bewildered also at the voice of instruction; if fearful thunder terrifies the deaf, then would fearful wrath stir the unclean!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: That the deaf hears not is no blame to him; but whoso tramples [on the commandments] it is headiness.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: From time to time there is thunder: but the voice of the law thunders every day.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let us not close our ears when their openings, as being opened and not closed against it, accuse us; and the door of hearing is open by nature, that it might reproach us for our headiness against our will.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The door of the voice and the door of the mouth our will can open or close.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Let us see what the Good One has given us; and let us hear the mighty Voice, and let not the doors of our ears be closed.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Glory to that Voice Which became Body, and to the Word of the High One Which became Flesh!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Hear Him also, O ears, and see Him, O eyes, and feel Him, O hands, and eat Him, O mouth!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Ye members and senses give praise unto Him, that came and quickened the whole body!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Mary bare the silent Babe, while in Him were hidden all tongues!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Joseph bare Him, and in Him was hidden a nature more ancient than aught that is old!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The High One became as a little child, and in Him was hidden a treasure of wisdom sufficing for all!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Though Most High, yet He sucked the milk of Mary, and of His goodness all creatures suck!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He is the Breast of Life, and the Breath of Life; the dead suck from His life and revive.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Without the breath of the air no man lives, without the Might of the Son no man subsists.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: On His living breath that quickeneth all, depend the spirits that are above and that are beneath.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: When He sucked the milk of Mary, He was suckling all with Life.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While He was lying on His Mother’s bosom, in His bosom were all creatures lying.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He was silent as a Babe, and yet He was making His creatures execute all His commands.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For without the First-born no man can approach unto the Essence, to which He is equal.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The thirty years He was in the earth, Who was ordering all creatures, Who was receiving all the offerings of praise from those above and those below.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He was wholly in the depths and wholly in the highest!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He was wholly with all things and wholly with each.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While His body was forming within the womb, His power was fashioning all members!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While the Conception of the Son was fashioning in the womb, He Himself was fashioning babes in the womb.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Yet not as His body was weak in the womb, was His power weak in the womb!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: So too not as His body was feeble by the Cross, was His might also feeble by the Cross.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For when on the Cross He quickened the dead, His Body quickened them, yea, rather His Will; just as when He was dwelling wholly in the womb, His hidden Will was visiting all!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For see how, when He was wholly hanging upon the Cross, His Power was yet making all creatures move!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For He darkened the sun and made the earth quake; He rent the graves and brought forth the dead!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: See how when He was wholly on the Cross, yet again He was wholly everywhere!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thus was He entirely in the womb, while He was again wholly in everything!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While on the Cross He quickened the dead, so while a Babe He was fashioning babes.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While He was slain, He opened the graves; while He was in the womb, He opened wombs.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Come hearken, my brethren, concerning the Son of the Secret One that was revealed in His Body, while His Power was concealed!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For the Power of the Son is a free Power; the womb did not bind it up, as it did the Body!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For while His Power was dwelling in the womb, He was fashioning infants in the womb!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: His Power compassed her, that compassed Him.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For if He drew in His Power, all things would fall; His Power upholds all things; while He was within the womb, He left not His hold of all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He in His own Person shaped an Image in the womb, and was shaping in all wombs all countenances.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Whilst He was increasing in stature among the poor, from an abundant treasury He was nourishing all!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: While she that anointed Him was anointing Him, with His dew and His rain He was anointing all!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Magi brought myrrh and gold, while in Him was hidden a treasure of riches.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The myrrh and spices which He had prepared and created, did the Magi bring Him of His own.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: It was by Power from Him that Mary was able to bear in Her bosom Him that bears up all things!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: It was from the great storehouse of all creatures, Mary gave Him all which she did give Him!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: She gave Him milk from Himself that prepared it, she gave Him food from Himself that made it!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He gave milk unto Mary as God: again He sucked it from her, as the Son of Man.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Her hands bare Him in that He had emptied His strength; and her arm embraced Him, in that He had made Himself small.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The measure of His Majesty who has measured?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He caused His measures to shrink into a Raiment.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: She wove for Him and clothed Him because He had put off His glory.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: She measured Him and wove for Him, since He had made Himself little.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The sea when it bore Him was still and calmed, and how came the lap of Joseph to bear Him?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The womb of hell conceived Him and was burst open, and how did the womb of Mary contain Him?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The stone that was over the grave He broke open by His might, and how could Mary’s arm contain Him?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Thou camest to a low estate, that Thou mightest raise all to life!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Glory be unto Thee from all that are quickened by Thee!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Who is able to speak of the Son of the Hidden One who came down and clothed Himself with a Body in the womb?

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He came forth and sucked milk as a child, and among little children the Son of the Lord of all crept about.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: They saw Him as a little Child in the street, while there was dwelling in Him the Love of all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Visibly children surrounded Him in the street; secretly Angels surrounded Him in fear.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Cheerful was He with the little ones as a child; awful was He with the Angels as a Commander: He was awful to John for him to loose His shoe’s latchet: He was gentle to sinners that kissed His feet!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Angels as Angels saw Him; according to the measure of his knowledge each man beheld Him: according to the measure of each man’s discernment, thus he perceived Him that is greater than all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: The Father and Himself alone are a full measure of knowledge so as know Him as He is!

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: For every creature whether above or below obtains each his measure of knowledge; He the Lord of all gives all to us.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He that enriches all, requires usury of all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He gives to all things as wanting nothing, and yet requires usury of all as if needy.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He gave us herds and flocks as Creator, and yet asked sacrifices as though in need.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He made the water wine as Maker: and yet he drank of it as a poor man.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Of His own He mingled [wine] in the marriage feast, His wine He mingled and gave to drink when He was a guest.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In His love He multiplied [the days of] the aged Simeon; that he, a mortal, might present Him who quickeneth all.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: By power from Him did Simeon carry Him; he that presented Him, was by Him presented [to God].

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: He gave imposition of hands to Moses in the Mount, and received it in the midst of the river from John.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: In the power of His gifts John was enabled to baptize, though earthy, the heavenly.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: By power from Him the earth supported Him: it was nigh to being dissolved, and His might strengthened it.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Martha gave Him to eat: viands which He had created she placed before Him.

verses.book: Ephraim Syrus: Nineteen Hymns on the Nativity of Christ in the Flesh.
verses.text: Of His own all that give have made their vows: of His own treasures they placed upon His table.
